entries.title: Pre-Dawn War-Prep — 2026-04-23
entries.rationale:
A single pre-dawn working session, driven by Aurelius on behalf of the Sovereign, bridging the gap between war preparation (Session closed 2026-04-23) and War Time Hour 0 (2026-04-24 08:00 IST).

Arc: the session opened with a status check on the already-merged pragmatic patch (SEP Dashboard v2.1 restore), surfaced the SproutLab LOC inflation bug during verification, cleaned the cruft (PR #2 on sproutlab: -77,226 lines), disposed of Codex PR #19 (the War Time declaration PR that was doc-only after its content had already landed on main — closed with pointer comment), then pivoted to the Temple of Mars founding.

Temple arc: four charter decisions (permanent-not-one-shot, live-Codex-fetch, SvelteKit stack, GH Pages hosting), metrics decision (Priority 1 + 2 via session_log snippet), 41-file scaffold streamed across 9 commits (after first attempt hit stream timeout), Codex integration (#20 campaigns.json + Session v2 schema), then a four-step deploy fire-drill that birthed three Cautionary Tales before going green at 23s.

The temple stands. The clock ticks. The Order is ready.

entries.title: The Founding of Temple of Mars
entries.rationale:
Temple of Mars became the second Monument-class artifact of the Order — sibling to the Command Center, proposed under a new structural class called `Watchtower`. It is the first repository in the Order to deliberately break from the single-file HTML PWA pattern, adopting Stack B (SvelteKit + adapter-static + Tailwind v4 + Vite-PWA + TypeScript strict + Biome + Vitest).

Builder: Ignis. First project. Per Sovereign decree 2026-04-23, Ignis vacated a Cabinet Minister seat to assume this Builder seat; formal canon-inst-* entry queued (chapter `ch-ignis-canon-entry`).

Inaugural function: campaign dashboard for War Time 2026-04-24. But Temple is permanent — the first campaign is not its only purpose. It will outlast the war, render Peacetime views, grow a Lore browser, archive campaigns, and stand as the Order's institutional-memory surface going forward.

Live at `https://rishabh1804.github.io/TempleOfMars/` as of 2026-04-23. Pillar I honored: every session logged, every decision captured, every discovery recorded. Nothing is wasted.
